   513→```
   514→</interfaces>
   515→```
   516→
   517→### For plans that CREATE new interfaces:
   518→If this plan creates types/interfaces that later plans depend on, include a "Wave 0" skeleton step:
   519→
   520→```xml
   521→<task type="auto">
   522→  <name>Task 0: Write interface contracts</name>
   523→  <files>src/types/newFeature.ts</files>
   524→  <action>Create type definitions that downstream plans will implement against. These are the contracts — implementation comes in later tasks.</action>
   525→  <verify>File exists with exported types, no implementation</verify>
   526→  <done>Interface file committed, types exported</done>
   527→</task>
   528→```
   529→
   530→### When to include interfaces:
   531→- Plan touches files that import from other modules → extract those module's exports
   532→- Plan creates a new API endpoint → extract the request/response types
   533→- Plan modifies a component → extract its props interface
   534→- Plan depends on a previous plan's output → extract the types from that plan's files_modified
   535→
   536→### When to skip:
   537→- Plan is self-contained (creates everything from scratch, no imports)
   538→- Plan is pure configuration (no code interfaces involved)
   539→- Level 0 discovery (all patterns already established)
   540→
   541→## Context Section Rules
   542→
   543→Only include prior plan SUMMARY references if genuinely needed (uses types/exports from prior plan, or prior plan made decision affecting this one).
   544→
   545→**Anti-pattern:** Reflexive chaining (02 refs 01, 03 refs 02...). Independent plans need NO prior SUMMARY references.
   546→
   547→## User Setup Frontmatter
   548→
   549→When external services involved:
   550→
   551→```yaml
   552→user_setup:
   553→  - service: stripe
   554→    why: "Payment processing"
   555→    env_vars:
   556→      - name: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY
   557→        source: "Stripe Dashboard -> Developers -> API keys"
   558→    dashboard_config:
   559→      - task: "Create webhook endpoint"
   560→        location: "Stripe Dashboard -> Developers -> Webhooks"
   561→```
   562→
   563→Only include what Claude literally cannot do.
   564→
   565→</plan_format>

   567→<goal_backward>
   568→
   569→## Goal-Backward Methodology
   570→
   571→**Forward planning:** "What should we build?" → produces tasks.
   572→**Goal-backward:** "What must be TRUE for the goal to be achieved?" → produces requirements tasks must satisfy.
   573→
   574→## The Process
   575→
   576→**Step 0: Extract Requirement IDs**
   577→Read ROADMAP.md `**Requirements:**` line for this phase. Strip brackets if present (e.g., `[AUTH-01, AUTH-02]` → `AUTH-01, AUTH-02`). Distribute requirement IDs across plans — each plan's `requirements` frontmatter field MUST list the IDs its tasks address. **CRITICAL:** Every requirement ID MUST appear in at least one plan. Plans with an empty `requirements` field are invalid.
   578→
   579→**Step 1: State the Goal**
   580→Take phase goal from ROADMAP.md. Must be outcome-shaped, not task-shaped.
   581→- Good: "Working chat interface" (outcome)
   582→- Bad: "Build chat components" (task)
   583→
   584→**Step 2: Derive Observable Truths**
   585→"What must be TRUE for this goal to be achieved?" List 3-7 truths from USER's perspective.
   586→
   587→For "working chat interface":
   588→- User can see existing messages
   589→- User can type a new message
   590→- User can send the message
   591→- Sent message appears in the list
   592→- Messages persist across page refresh
   593→
   594→**Test:** Each truth verifiable by a human using the application.
   595→
   596→**Step 3: Derive Required Artifacts**
   597→For each truth: "What must EXIST for this to be true?"
   598→
   599→"User can see existing messages" requires:
   600→- Message list component (renders Message[])
   601→- Messages state (loaded from somewhere)
   602→- API route or data source (provides messages)
   603→- Message type definition (shapes the data)
   604→
   605→**Test:** Each artifact = a specific file or database object.
   606→
   607→**Step 4: Derive Required Wiring**
   608→For each artifact: "What must be CONNECTED for this to function?"
   609→
   610→Message list component wiring:
   611→- Imports Message type (not using `any`)
   612→- Receives messages prop or fetches from API
   613→- Maps over messages to render (not hardcoded)
   614→- Handles empty state (not just crashes)
   615→
   616→**Step 5: Identify Key Links**
   617→"Where is this most likely to break?" Key links = critical connections where breakage causes cascading failures.
   618→
   619→For chat interface:
   620→- Input onSubmit -> API call (if broken: typing works but sending doesn't)
   621→- API save -> database (if broken: appears to send but doesn't persist)
   622→- Component -> real data (if broken: shows placeholder, not messages)
   623→
   624→## Must-Haves Output Format
   625→
   626→```yaml
   627→must_haves:
   628→  truths:
   629→    - "User can see existing messages"
   630→    - "User can send a message"
   631→    - "Messages persist across refresh"
   632→  artifacts:
   633→    - path: "src/components/Chat.tsx"
   634→      provides: "Message list rendering"
   635→      min_lines: 30
   636→    - path: "src/app/api/chat/route.ts"
   637→      provides: "Message CRUD operations"
   638→      exports: ["GET", "POST"]
   639→    - path: "prisma/schema.prisma"
   640→      provides: "Message model"
   641→      contains: "model Message"
   642→  key_links:
   643→    - from: "src/components/Chat.tsx"
   644→      to: "/api/chat"
   645→      via: "fetch in useEffect"
   646→      pattern: "fetch.*api/chat"
   647→    - from: "src/app/api/chat/route.ts"
   648→      to: "prisma.message"
   649→      via: "database query"
   650→      pattern: "prisma\\.message\\.(find|create)"
   651→```
   652→
   653→## Common Failures
   654→
   655→**Truths too vague:**
   656→- Bad: "User can use chat"
   657→- Good: "User can see messages", "User can send message", "Messages persist"
   658→
   659→**Artifacts too abstract:**
   660→- Bad: "Chat system", "Auth module"
   661→- Good: "src/components/Chat.tsx", "src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts"
   662→
   663→**Missing wiring:**
   664→- Bad: Listing components without how they connect
   665→- Good: "Chat.tsx fetches from /api/chat via useEffect on mount"
   666→
   667→</goal_backward>
   668→
   669→<checkpoints>
   670→
   671→## Checkpoint Types
   672→
   673→**checkpoint:human-verify (90% of checkpoints)**
   674→Human confirms Claude's automated work works correctly.
   675→
   676→Use for: Visual UI checks, interactive flows, functional verification, animation/accessibility.
   677→
   678→```xml
   679→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify" gate="blocking">
   680→  <what-built>[What Claude automated]</what-built>
   681→  <how-to-verify>
   682→    [Exact steps to test - URLs, commands, expected behavior]
   683→  </how-to-verify>
   684→  <resume-signal>Type "approved" or describe issues</resume-signal>
   685→</task>
   686→```
   687→
   688→**checkpoint:decision (9% of checkpoints)**
   689→Human makes implementation choice affecting direction.
   690→
   691→Use for: Technology selection, architecture decisions, design choices.
   692→
   693→```xml
   694→<task type="checkpoint:decision" gate="blocking">
   695→  <decision>[What's being decided]</decision>
   696→  <context>[Why this matters]</context>
   697→  <options>
   698→    <option id="option-a">
   699→      <name>[Name]</name>
   700→      <pros>[Benefits]</pros>
   701→      <cons>[Tradeoffs]</cons>
   702→    </option>
   703→  </options>
   704→  <resume-signal>Select: option-a, option-b, or ...</resume-signal>
   705→</task>
   706→```
   707→
   708→**checkpoint:human-action (1% - rare)**
   709→Action has NO CLI/API and requires human-only interaction.
   710→
   711→Use ONLY for: Email verification links, SMS 2FA codes, manual account approvals, credit card 3D Secure flows.
   712→
   713→Do NOT use for: Deploying (use CLI), creating webhooks (use API), creating databases (use provider CLI), running builds/tests (use Bash), creating files (use Write).
   714→
   715→## Authentication Gates
   716→
   717→When Claude tries CLI/API and gets auth error → creates checkpoint → user authenticates → Claude retries. Auth gates are created dynamically, NOT pre-planned.
   718→
   719→## Writing Guidelines
   720→
   721→**DO:** Automate everything before checkpoint, be specific ("Visit https://myapp.vercel.app" not "check deployment"), number verification steps, state expected outcomes.
   722→
   723→**DON'T:** Ask human to do work Claude can automate, mix multiple verifications, place checkpoints before automation completes.
   724→
   725→## Anti-Patterns
   726→
   727→**Bad - Asking human to automate:**
   728→```xml
   729→<task type="checkpoint:human-action">
   730→  <action>Deploy to Vercel</action>
   731→  <instructions>Visit vercel.com, import repo, click deploy...</instructions>
   732→</task>
   733→```
   734→Why bad: Vercel has a CLI. Claude should run `vercel --yes`.
   735→
   736→**Bad - Too many checkpoints:**
   737→```xml
   738→<task type="auto">Create schema</task>
   739→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify">Check schema</task>
   740→<task type="auto">Create API</task>
   741→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify">Check API</task>
   742→```
   743→Why bad: Verification fatigue. Combine into one checkpoint at end.
   744→
   745→**Good - Single verification checkpoint:**
   746→```xml
   747→<task type="auto">Create schema</task>
   748→<task type="auto">Create API</task>
   749→<task type="auto">Create UI</task>
   750→<task type="checkpoint:human-verify">
   751→  <what-built>Complete auth flow (schema + API + UI)</what-built>
   752→  <how-to-verify>Test full flow: register, login, access protected page</how-to-verify>
   753→</task>
   754→```
   755→
   756→</checkpoints>
   757→
   758→<tdd_integration>
   759→
   760→## TDD Plan Structure
   761→
   762→TDD candidates identified in task_breakdown get dedicated plans (type: tdd). One feature per TDD plan.
   763→
   764→```markdown
   765→---
   766→phase: XX-name
   767→plan: NN
   768→type: tdd
   769→---
   770→
   771→<objective>
   772→[What feature and why]
   773→Purpose: [Design benefit of TDD for this feature]
   774→Output: [Working, tested feature]
   775→</objective>
   776→
   777→<feature>
   778→  <name>[Feature name]</name>
   779→  <files>[source file, test file]</files>
   780→  <behavior>
   781→    [Expected behavior in testable terms]
   782→    Cases: input -> expected output
   783→  </behavior>
   784→  <implementation>[How to implement once tests pass]</implementation>
   785→</feature>
   786→```
   787→
   788→## Red-Green-Refactor Cycle
   789→
   790→**RED:** Create test file → write test describing expected behavior → run test (MUST fail) → commit: `test({phase}-{plan}): add failing test for [feature]`
   791→
   792→**GREEN:** Write minimal code to pass → run test (MUST pass) → commit: `feat({phase}-{plan}): implement [feature]`
   793→
   794→**REFACTOR (if needed):** Clean up → run tests (MUST pass) → commit: `refactor({phase}-{plan}): clean up [feature]`
   795→
   796→Each TDD plan produces 2-3 atomic commits.
   797→
   798→## Context Budget for TDD
   799→
   800→TDD plans target ~40% context (lower than standard 50%). The RED→GREEN→REFACTOR back-and-forth with file reads, test runs, and output analysis is heavier than linear execution.
   801→
   802→</tdd_integration>
   803→
   804→<gap_closure_mode>
   805→
   806→## Planning from Verification Gaps
   807→
   808→Triggered by `--gaps` flag. Creates plans to address verification or UAT failures.
   809→
   810→**1. Find gap sources:**
   811→
   812→Use init context (from load_project_state) which provides `phase_dir`:
   813→
   814→```bash
   815→# Check for VERIFICATION.md (code verification gaps)
   816→ls "$phase_dir"/*-VERIFICATION.md 2>/dev/null
   817→
   818→# Check for UAT.md with diagnosed status (user testing gaps)
   819→grep -l "status: diagnosed" "$phase_dir"/*-UAT.md 2>/dev/null
   820→```
   821→
   822→**2. Parse gaps:** Each gap has: truth (failed behavior), reason, artifacts (files with issues), missing (things to add/fix).
   823→
   824→**3. Load existing SUMMARYs** to understand what's already built.
   825→
   826→**4. Find next plan number:** If plans 01-03 exist, next is 04.
   827→
   828→**5. Group gaps into plans** by: same artifact, same concern, dependency order (can't wire if artifact is stub → fix stub first).
   829→
   830→**6. Create gap closure tasks:**
   831→
   832→```xml
   833→<task name="{fix_description}" type="auto">
   834→  <files>{artifact.path}</files>
   835→  <action>
   836→    {For each item in gap.missing:}
   837→    - {missing item}
   838→
   839→    Reference existing code: {from SUMMARYs}
   840→    Gap reason: {gap.reason}
   841→  </action>
   842→  <verify>{How to confirm gap is closed}</verify>
   843→  <done>{Observable truth now achievable}</done>
   844→</task>
   845→```
   846→
   847→**7. Write PLAN.md files:**
   848→
   849→```yaml
   850→---
   851→phase: XX-name
   852→plan: NN              # Sequential after existing
   853→type: execute
   854→wave: 1               # Gap closures typically single wave
   855→depends_on: []
   856→files_modified: [...]
   857→autonomous: true
   858→gap_closure: true     # Flag for tracking
   859→---
   860→```
   861→
   862→</gap_closure_mode>
   863→
   864→<revision_mode>
   865→
   866→## Planning from Checker Feedback
   867→
   868→Triggered when orchestrator provides `<revision_context>` with checker issues. NOT starting fresh — making targeted updates to existing plans.
   869→
   870→**Mindset:** Surgeon, not architect. Minimal changes for specific issues.
   871→
   872→### Step 1: Load Existing Plans
   873→
   874→```bash
   875→cat .planning/phases/$PHASE-*/$PHASE-*-PLAN.md
   876→```
   877→
   878→Build mental model of current plan structure, existing tasks, must_haves.
   879→
   880→### Step 2: Parse Checker Issues
   881→
   882→Issues come in structured format:
   883→
   884→```yaml
   885→issues:
   886→  - plan: "16-01"
   887→    dimension: "task_completeness"
   888→    severity: "blocker"
   889→    description: "Task 2 missing <verify> element"
   890→    fix_hint: "Add verification command for build output"
   891→```
   892→
   893→Group by plan, dimension, severity.
   894→
   895→### Step 3: Revision Strategy
   896→
   897→| Dimension | Strategy |
   898→|-----------|----------|
   899→| requirement_coverage | Add task(s) for missing requirement |
   900→| task_completeness | Add missing elements to existing task |
   901→| dependency_correctness | Fix depends_on, recompute waves |
   902→| key_links_planned | Add wiring task or update action |

```typescript
// Runtime-resolved API base URL (fetched once from /api/config)
let _configPromise: Promise<string> | null = null;

function getApiBase(): Promise<string> {
  if (!_configPromise) {
    _configPromise =
      typeof window !== "undefined"
        ? fetch("/api/config")
            .then((r) => r.json())
            .then((d) => d.apiBaseUrl as string)
            .catch(() => "http://localhost:5200")
        : Promise.resolve(
            process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "http://localhost:5200"
          );
  }
  return _configPromise;
}
```

Key design decisions:
- **Promise-based singleton**: Caches the Promise itself (not the resolved value) to prevent race conditions when multiple components mount simultaneously
- **`typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard**: Prevents SSR from trying to fetch its own route handler via HTTP
- **Server-side fallback**: Uses `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` for backward compatibility in SSR, falls back to localhost for local dev
- **catch fallback**: If `/api/config` fails (e.g., offline dev), falls back to localhost:5200

3. **Mechanically replace every `${API_BASE}` usage** in every function with:
   - Add `const apiBase = await getApiBase();` as the FIRST line of every async function that uses the base URL
   - Replace `${API_BASE}` with `${apiBase}`

There are approximately 35+ functions that use `API_BASE`. Every single one must be updated. The change is purely mechanical -- the function signatures, return types, and behavior remain identical. The hooks in `src/hooks/` already call these functions via TanStack Query's `queryFn` which expects Promise returns, so NO hook changes are needed.

Example transformation (apply to ALL functions):

Before:
```typescript
export async function getProducts(params: GetProductsParams = {}): Promise<ProductListDto> {
  // ... build searchParams ...
  const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/catalog/products?${searchParams}`, {
```

After:
```typescript
export async function getProducts(params: GetProductsParams = {}): Promise<ProductListDto> {
  const apiBase = await getApiBase();
  // ... build searchParams ...
  const response = await fetch(`${apiBase}/api/catalog/products?${searchParams}`, {
```

DO NOT change any interfaces, types, function signatures, error handling, or request options. Only the URL resolution changes.

DO NOT modify any files in `src/hooks/` -- they already work with the async API functions.

- **Mechanical refactor only**: All 35+ API functions get `const apiBase = await getApiBase();` as first line; no signature, type, or behavior changes; hooks need zero changes
- **120s timeout for observability pods**: Matches other non-infrastructure service timeouts in bootstrap.sh

### Next Steps

Execute: `/gsd:execute-phase 29`

    78→  <action>
    79→Refactor `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/lib/api.ts` with these exact changes:
    80→
    81→1. **Delete line 1** (`const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:5200';`)
    82→
    83→2. **Add the runtime config resolver** at the top of the file (before any interfaces):
    84→
    85→```typescript
    86→// Runtime-resolved API base URL (fetched once from /api/config)
    87→let _configPromise: Promise<string> | null = null;
    88→
    89→function getApiBase(): Promise<string> {
    90→  if (!_configPromise) {
    91→    _configPromise =
    92→      typeof window !== "undefined"
    93→        ? fetch("/api/config")
    94→            .then((r) => r.json())
    95→            .then((d) => d.apiBaseUrl as string)
    96→            .catch(() => "http://localhost:5200")
    97→        : Promise.resolve(
    98→            process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || "http://localhost:5200"
    99→          );
   100→  }
   101→  return _configPromise;
   102→}
   103→```
   104→
   105→Key design decisions:
   106→- **Promise-based singleton**: Caches the Promise itself (not the resolved value) to prevent race conditions when multiple components mount simultaneously
   107→- **`typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard**: Prevents SSR from trying to fetch its own route handler via HTTP
   108→- **Server-side fallback**: Uses `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` for backward compatibility in SSR, falls back to localhost for local dev
   109→- **catch fallback**: If `/api/config` fails (e.g., offline dev), falls back to localhost:5200
   110→
   111→3. **Mechanically replace every `${API_BASE}` usage** in every function with:
   112→   - Add `const apiBase = await getApiBase();` as the FIRST line of every async function that uses the base URL
   113→   - Replace `${API_BASE}` with `${apiBase}`
   114→
   115→There are approximately 35+ functions that use `API_BASE`. Every single one must be updated. The change is purely mechanical -- the function signatures, return types, and behavior remain identical. The hooks in `src/hooks/` already call these functions via TanStack Query's `queryFn` which expects Promise returns, so NO hook changes are needed.

   122→  // ... build searchParams ...
   123→  const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/catalog/products?${searchParams}`, {
   124→```
   125→
   126→After:

   129→  const apiBase = await getApiBase();
   130→  // ... build searchParams ...
   131→  const response = await fetch(`${apiBase}/api/catalog/products?${searchParams}`, {
   132→```
   133→
   134→DO NOT change any interfaces, types, function signatures, error handling, or request options. Only the URL resolution changes.
   135→
   136→DO NOT modify any files in `src/hooks/` -- they already work with the async API functions.
   137→  </action>

    58→### 🚧 v3.0 Kubernetes & GitOps (In Progress)
    59→
    60→**Milestone Goal:** Deploy the full MicroCommerce stack to a kind-based Kubernetes cluster using GitOps via ArgoCD, with GitHub Actions CI building and pushing images to ghcr.io, Kustomize managing manifests, and Sealed Secrets handling credentials safely in Git.
    61→
    62→- [x] **Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline** - Build production-ready images for all three services and push them to ghcr.io via GitHub Actions (completed 2026-02-25)
    63→- [x] **Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets** - Deploy PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak in the kind cluster with Sealed Secrets for all credentials (completed 2026-02-26)
    64→- [x] **Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport** - Deploy ApiService, Gateway, and Web with Kustomize base/overlay structure and RabbitMQ transport support (completed 2026-02-26)
    65→- [x] **Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps** - Install ArgoCD and wire app-of-apps to manage all cluster resources from Git (completed 2026-03-02)
    66→- [x] **Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure** - Extend CI to commit SHA image tags back to the overlay, completing the full GitOps loop (completed 2026-03-02)
    67→- [x] **Phase 28: Observability** - Deploy OTEL Collector and standalone Aspire Dashboard for in-cluster monitoring (completed 2026-03-02)
    68→- [ ] **Phase 29: K8s Client-Side API & Bootstrap Polish** - Fix client-side API wiring for K8s, polish bootstrap script, align REQUIREMENTS.md (gap closure)
    69→
    70→## Phase Details
    71→
    72→### Phase 23: Dockerfiles and Container Image Pipeline
    73→**Goal**: Three production-ready container images exist in ghcr.io and are built automatically on every push to master
    74→**Depends on**: Phase 22 (v2.0 complete)
    75→**Requirements**: CONT-01, CONT-02, CONT-03, CONT-04, CICD-01
    76→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    77→  1. `docker build` succeeds for ApiService, Gateway, and Web with no warnings or root-user violations
    78→  2. `docker run` starts each service and the process responds to health checks within 30 seconds
    79→  3. A push to master triggers the GitHub Actions workflow and all three images appear in ghcr.io tagged with the commit SHA
    80→  4. Image sizes are reasonable: .NET images use chiseled runtime and Next.js uses standalone output with node:alpine
    81→**Plans**: TBD
    82→
    83→### Phase 24: Infrastructure Manifests and Secrets
    84→**Goal**: PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ, and Keycloak run stably in the kind cluster with all credentials sealed in Git
    85→**Depends on**: Phase 23
    86→**Requirements**: INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03, INFRA-04, INFRA-05, GOPS-03
    87→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
    88→  1. `kind create cluster --config kind-config.yaml` creates a cluster and all infrastructure pods reach Running state
    89→  2. PostgreSQL data persists across pod restarts (PVC-backed StatefulSet)
    90→  3. Keycloak admin UI is reachable and the MicroCommerce realm is pre-loaded from a ConfigMap
    91→  4. No plaintext credentials exist in any committed YAML file; all secrets are SealedSecret objects
    92→  5. Keycloak startup probe is configured with sufficient timeout (15s delay + 60s probing window) to survive slow first-boot realm import
    93→**Plans**: 4 plans
    94→- [x] 24-01-PLAN.md -- Kind cluster config, Kustomize scaffold, PostgreSQL StatefulSet
    95→- [x] 24-02-PLAN.md -- RabbitMQ Deployment, Keycloak Deployment with realm import
    96→- [x] 24-03-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script with SealedSecrets integration
    97→- [x] 24-04-PLAN.md -- Gap closure: fix ROADMAP misalignment on success criterion #5
    98→
    99→### Phase 25: Application Manifests and MassTransit Transport
   100→**Goal**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web are deployed via Kustomize and communicate correctly using K8s DNS and RabbitMQ messaging
   101→**Depends on**: Phase 24
   102→**Requirements**: MFST-01, MFST-02, MFST-03, MFST-04, MFST-05, MFST-06, TRAN-01, TRAN-02
   103→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   104→  1. The storefront loads in a browser via the Gateway NodePort — browsing products and adding to cart works end-to-end
   105→  2. Checkout completes successfully using the RabbitMQ transport (saga orchestrates stock reservation and mock payment)
   106→  3. All pods report liveness and readiness as healthy (`kubectl get pods -n micro-commerce` shows all Running/Ready)
   107→  4. All resources are in the `micro-commerce` namespace and have CPU/memory resource limits set
   108→  5. Setting `MASSTRANSIT_TRANSPORT=AzureServiceBus` (Aspire dev path) and `RabbitMQ` (K8s path) both produce working configurations
   109→  6. ApiService startup probe prevents liveness failure during slow first-boot EF Core migration (deferred from Phase 24 INFRA-05)
   110→**Plans**: 3 plans

   136→### Phase 26: ArgoCD GitOps
   137→**Goal**: ArgoCD manages all cluster resources from Git using app-of-apps, replacing manual kubectl apply
   138→**Depends on**: Phase 25

   140→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):

   142→  2. Deleting a deployment manually causes ArgoCD to automatically restore it within one sync cycle
   143→  3. The app-of-apps root Application manages each service as an independent child Application
   144→**Plans**: 2 plans

   146→- [ ] 26-02-PLAN.md -- Bootstrap script ArgoCD integration and GitOps handoff

   148→### Phase 27: CI/CD GitOps Loop Closure
   149→**Goal**: A push to master automatically flows through CI image build, Git tag commit, and ArgoCD cluster rollout without manual intervention
   150→**Depends on**: Phase 26
   151→**Requirements**: CICD-02
   152→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   153→  1. After a push to master, a new pod with the updated SHA-tagged image is running in the cluster within 5 minutes of the CI workflow completing
   154→  2. Every deployment is traceable to a Git commit — the image tag in the overlay matches the commit SHA that triggered the build
   155→**Plans**: TBD

   157→### Phase 28: Observability
   158→**Goal**: Traces, metrics, and logs from all cluster services are visible in the Aspire Dashboard without requiring .NET Aspire AppHost
   159→**Depends on**: Phase 27
   160→**Requirements**: OBSV-01, OBSV-02
   161→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   162→  1. The Aspire Dashboard UI is reachable in the kind cluster and shows live traces from ApiService and Gateway
   163→  2. A completed checkout flow produces a distributed trace visible end-to-end in the Dashboard spanning ApiService, the saga, and RabbitMQ consumers
   164→  3. OTEL Collector pod stays healthy under normal load (no OOM kills, no dropped telemetry in logs)
   165→**Plans**: 2 plans

    22→- [x] **INFRA-04**: kind cluster can be created from a config file with port mappings for local access

    24→
    25→### Application Manifests
    26→
    27→- [x] **MFST-01**: ApiService, Gateway, and Web have Deployment + Service + ConfigMap manifests

    96→## Out of Scope
    97→
    98→| Feature | Reason |
    99→|---------|--------|
   100→| Service Mesh (Istio/Linkerd) | YARP Gateway already centralizes auth/routing; massive operational complexity |
   101→| Helm charts | Kustomize is simpler for single-project, built into kubectl |
   102→| External Secrets Operator | Requires external vault; Sealed Secrets works offline with kind |
   103→| FluxCD | ArgoCD already chosen; switching tools is waste |
   104→| Multi-environment overlays | Only dev cluster exists; structure repo for easy addition later |
   105→| Ingress NGINX | EOL March 2026; use NodePort for kind local access |
   106→| Azure Blob Storage in K8s | Azurite emulator not suitable for K8s; use placeholder images |

1→# Phase 29: K8s Client-Side API & Bootstrap Polish - Research
     2→
     3→**Researched:** 2026-03-02
     4→**Domain:** Next.js runtime configuration, Kubernetes bootstrap scripting, documentation alignment
     5→**Confidence:** HIGH
     6→
     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→Phase 29 closes three gaps identified in the v3.0 milestone audit: (1) client-side API calls in the Next.js storefront hardcode `localhost:5200` and are unreachable when the app runs in K8s, (2) the bootstrap script does not wait for observability pods before declaring readiness, and (3) the REQUIREMENTS.md file has unchecked OBSV checkboxes and missing UI-* traceability rows.
    10→
    11→The client-side API issue (MISSING-02, high severity) is the most architecturally significant. The root cause is in `src/MicroCommerce.Web/src/lib/api.ts` line 1: `const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:5200'`. Because `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` variables are inlined at build time by Next.js, and no value is set during Docker build or K8s deployment, all 40+ client components and 6 TanStack Query hooks that import from `@/lib/api` make browser fetch calls to `localhost:5200` -- unreachable from a browser pointed at the K8s cluster.
    12→
    13→The project already has the correct pattern for server-side API calls: `src/lib/config.ts` reads `services__gateway__https__0` at runtime and an `/api/config` route handler exposes it. The fix is to extend this pattern so `api.ts` fetches the gateway URL at runtime from the `/api/config` route rather than relying on a build-time `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` variable.
    14→
    15→**Primary recommendation:** Replace the build-time `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` constant in `api.ts` with a runtime-resolved URL fetched from the existing `/api/config` server route, then add observability pod waits + Aspire Dashboard URL to bootstrap.sh, and update REQUIREMENTS.md checkboxes and traceability table.
    16→

    23→| K8S-BOOT-01 | bootstrap.sh waits for observability pods and prints Aspire Dashboard URL in access info | Bootstrap section: two `kubectl wait` commands + one `echo` line; labels `app=otel-collector` and `app=aspire-dashboard` already exist in manifests |
    24→| K8S-DOCS-01 | REQUIREMENTS.md checkboxes and traceability table reflect all delivered work | Documentation section: OBSV-01/OBSV-02 checkboxes, 11 UI-* traceability rows to add |

    27→## Standard Stack
    28→
    29→### Core
    30→
    31→| Library | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    32→|---------|---------|---------|--------------|

    34→| TanStack React Query | 5.90.20 | Client-side data fetching hooks (already in project) | All hooks in `src/hooks/` use it; no change needed to hooks themselves |
    35→

    42→### Alternatives Considered
    43→
    44→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
    45→|------------|-----------|----------|
    46→| `/api/config` route for runtime URL | `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` set at build time via Docker `--build-arg` | Build-time approach bakes URL into the JS bundle; different environments need different images; runtime approach uses single image across environments |
    47→| `/api/config` route for runtime URL | Next.js `publicRuntimeConfig` | Removed in Next.js 16 (see Context7: "serverRuntimeConfig and publicRuntimeConfig have been removed") |
    48→| `/api/config` route for runtime URL | `__NEXT_DATA__` injection via custom `_document` | Pages Router pattern, not applicable to App Router |
    49→
    50→**No new dependencies needed.** All three requirements are achievable with existing project tooling.
    51→
    52→## Architecture Patterns
    53→
    54→### Pattern 1: Runtime API Base URL via Server Route Handler
    55→
    56→**What:** Instead of `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` (inlined at build time), client-side code fetches the API base URL at runtime from a Next.js API route that reads server-side environment variables.
    57→
    58→**When to use:** When the same Docker image must work in multiple environments (Aspire local dev, kind K8s cluster, CI) without rebuilding.
    59→
    60→**Current broken pattern (api.ts line 1):**
    61→```typescript
    62→// BUILD-TIME: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is baked into the JS bundle during `next build`
    63→// In Docker/K8s: not set, so falls back to localhost:5200 (unreachable from browser)
    64→const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:5200';
    65→```
    66→
    67→**Fix pattern -- lazy-initialized runtime URL:**
    68→```typescript
    69→// Runtime-resolved API base URL
    70→// Fetched once from /api/config (server route that reads env vars at runtime)
    71→let _apiBase: string | null = null;
    72→
    73→async function getApiBase(): Promise<string> {
    74→  if (_apiBase) return _apiBase;
    75→
    76→  // In browser: fetch from server route
    77→  if (typeof window !== 'undefined') {
    78→    try {
    79→      const res = await fetch('/api/config');
    80→      const data = await res.json();
    81→      _apiBase = data.apiBaseUrl;
    82→      return _apiBase!;
    83→    } catch {
    84→      // Fallback for local dev without Aspire
    85→      _apiBase = 'http://localhost:5200';
    86→      return _apiBase;
    87→    }
    88→  }
    89→
    90→  // On server: use config.ts directly (import at top of file)
    91→  // This path is only hit during SSR, not in client components
    92→  _apiBase = 'http://localhost:5200';
    93→  return _apiBase;
    94→}
    95→```
    96→
    97→**Why this works:**
    98→- The existing `/api/config` route handler (`src/app/api/config/route.ts`) already reads `services__gateway__https__0` via `getApiBaseUrl()` from `config.ts`
    99→- In K8s, the Web deployment already has `services__gateway__https__0=http://gateway:8080` set
   100→- The API route runs server-side at request time, so it reads the runtime env var
   101→- Client code fetches `/api/config` once and caches the result
   102→
   103→**Impact radius:** All functions in `api.ts` use `API_BASE`. Every function signature changes from sync to async (they already return Promises, but the URL resolution becomes async). The hooks in `src/hooks/` call these functions via `queryFn` which already expects Promise returns, so **hooks need zero changes**.
   104→
   105→### Pattern 2: Singleton Module-Level Fetch with Promise Caching
   106→
   107→**What:** Use a module-level promise to ensure the config fetch happens exactly once and all concurrent callers share the same result.
   108→
   109→```typescript
   110→let _configPromise: Promise<string> | null = null;
   111→
   112→function getApiBase(): Promise<string> {
   113→  if (!_configPromise) {
   114→    _configPromise = (typeof window !== 'undefined')
   115→      ? fetch('/api/config')
   116→          .then(r => r.json())
   117→          .then(d => d.apiBaseUrl as string)
   118→          .catch(() => 'http://localhost:5200')
   119→      : Promise.resolve('http://localhost:5200');
   120→  }
   121→  return _configPromise;
   122→}
   123→```
   124→
   125→This is cleaner than the mutable variable pattern because:
   126→- No race condition: concurrent calls all await the same promise
   127→- Fetch happens exactly once per page load
   128→- Module-level caching persists across component renders
   129→
   130→### Pattern 3: Bootstrap Script Pod Wait
   131→
   132→**What:** Use `kubectl wait --for=condition=ready` with existing pod labels.
   133→
   134→```bash
   135→info "Waiting for OTEL Collector..."
   136→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=otel-collector -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   137→
   138→info "Waiting for Aspire Dashboard..."
   139→kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app=aspire-dashboard -n "$NAMESPACE" --timeout=120s
   140→```
   141→
   142→**Placement:** Between the current "Waiting for Web..." block (line 160) and the "Print access info" section (line 163). This ensures observability pods are ready before printing "Full stack ready!".
   143→
   144→**Aspire Dashboard URL in access info:**
   145→```bash
   146→echo "  Aspire Dashboard:    http://localhost:38888"
   147→```
   148→
   149→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   150→
   151→- **Setting `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` as a Docker build-arg:** Bakes the URL into the JS bundle at build time. Breaks the "one image, many environments" principle. Would require rebuilding the image for each environment.
   152→- **Using `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` in the K8s Deployment env:** `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` vars are inlined by webpack during `next build`, not read at runtime. Setting them in K8s has NO effect.
   153→- **Calling `/api/config` on every single fetch:** Wasteful. The URL does not change during a session. Fetch once, cache in module scope.
   154→- **Using `window.__NEXT_DATA__`:** Pages Router pattern, not available in App Router.
   155→
   156→## Don't Hand-Roll
   157→
   158→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   159→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   160→| Runtime config for client | Custom service worker or global script injection | Existing `/api/config` route handler | Route already exists, already reads `getApiBaseUrl()`, already works in K8s |
   161→| Pod readiness checking | Custom health polling loop | `kubectl wait --for=condition=ready` | Built into kubectl, handles timeout, exit code indicates success/failure |
   162→| Config caching | React context provider or localStorage | Module-level promise singleton | Simpler, works outside React tree, no provider wrapping needed |
   163→
   164→**Key insight:** The project already has 90% of the solution in place. The `/api/config` route and `config.ts` server-side resolution work correctly. The only gap is that `api.ts` ignores them and uses a build-time constant instead.
   165→
   166→## Common Pitfalls
   167→
   168→### Pitfall 1: NEXT_PUBLIC_ Variables Are Build-Time Only
   169→**What goes wrong:** Developer sets `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL` in K8s Deployment env vars expecting it to work at runtime. Client-side code still calls `localhost:5200`.
   170→**Why it happens:** Next.js webpack replaces `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_*` with literal string values during `next build`. The runtime environment is irrelevant.
   171→**How to avoid:** Never rely on `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` for values that differ between environments. Use server-side route handlers to expose runtime config to the client.
   172→**Warning signs:** Client-side fetch calls fail with `net::ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED` to localhost when running in K8s.
   173→
   174→### Pitfall 2: Race Condition on Config Fetch
   175→**What goes wrong:** Multiple components mount simultaneously, each triggering a separate `/api/config` fetch before the first one completes.
   176→**Why it happens:** Module-level mutable variable (`let _apiBase = null`) without promise-based deduplication.
   177→**How to avoid:** Cache the Promise itself, not just the resolved value. All concurrent callers await the same promise.
   178→**Warning signs:** Network tab shows multiple `/api/config` requests on initial page load.
   179→
   180→### Pitfall 3: Server-Side Rendering Trying to Fetch /api/config
   181→**What goes wrong:** During SSR, the `fetch('/api/config')` call fails because the server cannot call its own route handler via HTTP (or uses wrong port/host).
   182→**Why it happens:** `api.ts` functions can be called from both server and client contexts.
   183→**How to avoid:** Guard with `typeof window !== 'undefined'` check. On the server side, use `getApiBaseUrl()` directly from `config.ts` or fall back to a sensible default. Server components should not call client-side API functions anyway -- they use `config.ts` directly.
   184→**Warning signs:** SSR errors about fetch failing, or `ECONNREFUSED` in server logs during page render.
   185→
   186→### Pitfall 4: Forgetting to Await the New Async getApiBase()
   187→**What goes wrong:** After refactoring, a function calls `getApiBase()` without `await`, getting a Promise object instead of a URL string.
   188→**Why it happens:** The old `API_BASE` was a sync constant; the new one is async.
   189→**How to avoid:** Every function in `api.ts` that uses the base URL must `await getApiBase()`. Since all these functions already return Promises (they do `await fetch(...)`) and the hooks use them as `queryFn`, this is a natural fit.
   190→**Warning signs:** Fetch URLs like `[object Promise]/api/catalog/products` in network requests.
   191→
   192→### Pitfall 5: Bootstrap Pod Labels Not Matching
   193→**What goes wrong:** `kubectl wait` hangs or fails because the label selector does not match any pods.
   194→**Why it happens:** Using wrong label or namespace.
   195→**How to avoid:** Verify labels match exactly: `app=otel-collector` and `app=aspire-dashboard` (confirmed in deployment YAML metadata.labels). Use `--namespace=$NAMESPACE`.
   196→**Warning signs:** `kubectl wait` timeout with "no matching resources found".
   197→
   198→## Code Examples
   199→
   200→### api.ts Refactored Module Header
   201→
   202→```typescript
   203→// src/lib/api.ts
   204→// Runtime-resolved API base URL (fetched once from /api/config)
   205→let _configPromise: Promise<string> | null = null;
   206→
   207→function getApiBase(): Promise<string> {
   208→  if (!_configPromise) {
   209→    _configPromise = (typeof window !== 'undefined')
   210→      ? fetch('/api/config')
   211→          .then(r => r.json())
   212→          .then(d => d.apiBaseUrl as string)
   213→          .catch(() => 'http://localhost:5200')
   214→      : Promise.resolve(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:5200');
   215→  }
   216→  return _configPromise;
   217→}
   218→```
   219→
   220→### Example API Function Refactoring
   221→
   222→Before:
   223→```typescript
   224→export async function getProducts(params: GetProductsParams = {}): Promise<ProductListDto> {
   225→  // ... build searchParams ...
   226→  const response = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/catalog/products?${searchParams}`, {
   227→    cache: 'no-store',
   228→  });
   229→  // ...
   230→}
   231→```
   232→
   233→After:
   234→```typescript
   235→export async function getProducts(params: GetProductsParams = {}): Promise<ProductListDto> {
   236→  const apiBase = await getApiBase();
   237→  // ... build searchParams ...
   238→  const response = await fetch(`${apiBase}/api/catalog/products?${searchParams}`, {
   239→    cache: 'no-store',
   240→  });
   241→  // ...
   242→}
   243→```
   244→
   245→The change is mechanical: every function that uses `API_BASE` gets `const apiBase = await getApiBase();` as its first line, then replaces `${API_BASE}` with `${apiBase}`.

   270→## State of the Art
   271→
   272→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   273→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   274→| `publicRuntimeConfig` in next.config.js | Removed in Next.js 16 | Next.js 16 (2025) | Must use env vars or API routes for runtime config |
   275→| `NEXT_PUBLIC_*` for dynamic URLs | Server route handlers exposing runtime config | Next.js App Router pattern | Build-time inlining means NEXT_PUBLIC is unsuitable for multi-environment Docker images |
   276→| `getServerSideProps` for config injection | Server Components + Route Handlers | Next.js 13+ App Router | App Router does not have getServerSideProps |
   277→
   278→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   279→- `publicRuntimeConfig`/`serverRuntimeConfig`: Removed in Next.js 16. The project uses Next.js 16.0.3.
   280→- `getServerSideProps`/`getStaticProps`: Pages Router only. Project uses App Router.
   281→
   282→## Open Questions
   283→
   284→1. **Should `api-test.tsx` component also be updated?**
   285→   - What we know: `api-test.tsx` already fetches from `/api/config` and uses the dynamic URL. It has its own pattern.
   286→   - What's unclear: Whether it should be consolidated with the new `api.ts` pattern.
   287→   - Recommendation: Leave it as-is. It is a debug/test component, not part of the storefront. Low priority, can be cleaned up later.
   288→
   289→2. **CORS configuration for /api/config in K8s**
   290→   - What we know: `/api/config` is a Next.js API route served by the Web container itself. Client fetches it from the same origin (no CORS needed).
   291→   - What's unclear: Nothing -- this is a same-origin request.
   292→   - Recommendation: No CORS changes needed. The `/api/config` route is served by the same Next.js server that serves the page.

     2→name: nextjs-best-practices
     3→description: Next.js App Router principles. Server Components, data fetching, routing patterns.
     4→allowed-tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep
     5→license: MIT
     6→metadata:
     7→  version: "1.0.0"
     8→  domain: frontend
     9→  triggers: Next.js, App Router, Server Components, data fetching, routing
    10→  role: specialist
    11→  scope: implementation
    12→  output-format: code
    13→  related-skills: nextjs-app-router-patterns, nextjs-developer, react-expert
    14→---
    15→
    16→# Next.js Best Practices
    17→
    18→> Principles for Next.js App Router development.
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## 1. Server vs Client Components
    23→
    24→### Decision Tree
    25→
    26→```
    27→Does it need...?
    28→│
    29→├── useState, useEffect, event handlers
    30→│   └── Client Component ('use client')
    31→│
    32→├── Direct data fetching, no interactivity
    33→│   └── Server Component (default)
    34→│
    35→└── Both? 
    36→    └── Split: Server parent + Client child
    37→```
    38→
    39→### By Default
    40→
    41→| Type | Use |
    42→|------|-----|
    43→| **Server** | Data fetching, layout, static content |
    44→| **Client** | Forms, buttons, interactive UI |
    45→
    46→---
    47→
    48→## 2. Data Fetching Patterns
    49→
    50→### Fetch Strategy
    51→
    52→| Pattern | Use |
    53→|---------|-----|
    54→| **Default** | Static (cached at build) |
    55→| **Revalidate** | ISR (time-based refresh) |
    56→| **No-store** | Dynamic (every request) |
    57→
    58→### Data Flow
    59→
    60→| Source | Pattern |
    61→|--------|---------|
    62→| Database | Server Component fetch |
    63→| API | fetch with caching |
    64→| User input | Client state + server action |
    65→
    66→---
    67→
    68→## 3. Routing Principles
    69→
    70→### File Conventions
    71→
    72→| File | Purpose |
    73→|------|---------|
    74→| `page.tsx` | Route UI |
    75→| `layout.tsx` | Shared layout |
    76→| `loading.tsx` | Loading state |
    77→| `error.tsx` | Error boundary |
    78→| `not-found.tsx` | 404 page |
    79→
    80→### Route Organization
    81→
    82→| Pattern | Use |
    83→|---------|-----|
    84→| Route groups `(name)` | Organize without URL |
    85→| Parallel routes `@slot` | Multiple same-level pages |
    86→| Intercepting `(.)` | Modal overlays |
    87→
    88→---
    89→
    90→## 4. API Routes
    91→
    92→### Route Handlers
    93→
    94→| Method | Use |
    95→|--------|-----|
    96→| GET | Read data |
    97→| POST | Create data |
    98→| PUT/PATCH | Update data |
    99→| DELETE | Remove data |
   100→
   101→### Best Practices
   102→
   103→- Validate input with Zod
   104→- Return proper status codes
   105→- Handle errors gracefully
   106→- Use Edge runtime when possible
   107→
   108→---
   109→
   110→## 5. Performance Principles
   111→
   112→### Image Optimization
   113→
   114→- Use next/image component
   115→- Set priority for above-fold
   116→- Provide blur placeholder
   117→- Use responsive sizes
   118→
   119→### Bundle Optimization
   120→
   121→- Dynamic imports for heavy components
   122→- Route-based code splitting (automatic)
   123→- Analyze with bundle analyzer
   124→
   125→---
   126→
   127→## 6. Metadata
   128→
   129→### Static vs Dynamic
   130→
   131→| Type | Use |
   132→|------|-----|
   133→| Static export | Fixed metadata |
   134→| generateMetadata | Dynamic per-route |
   135→
   136→### Essential Tags
   137→
   138→- title (50-60 chars)
   139→- description (150-160 chars)
   140→- Open Graph images
   141→- Canonical URL
   142→
   143→---
   144→
   145→## 7. Caching Strategy
   146→
   147→### Cache Layers
   148→
   149→| Layer | Control |
   150→|-------|---------|
   151→| Request | fetch options |
   152→| Data | revalidate/tags |
   153→| Full route | route config |
   154→
   155→### Revalidation
   156→
   157→| Method | Use |
   158→|--------|-----|
   159→| Time-based | `revalidate: 60` |
   160→| On-demand | `revalidatePath/Tag` |
   161→| No cache | `no-store` |
   162→
   163→---
   164→
   165→## 8. Server Actions
   166→
   167→### Use Cases
   168→
   169→- Form submissions
   170→- Data mutations
   171→- Revalidation triggers
   172→
   173→### Best Practices
   174→
   175→- Mark with 'use server'
   176→- Validate all inputs
   177→- Return typed responses
   178→- Handle errors
   179→
   180→---
   181→
   182→## 9. Anti-Patterns
   183→
   184→| ❌ Don't | ✅ Do |
   185→|----------|-------|
   186→| 'use client' everywhere | Server by default |
   187→| Fetch in client components | Fetch in server |
   188→| Skip loading states | Use loading.tsx |
   189→| Ignore error boundaries | Use error.tsx |
   190→| Large client bundles | Dynamic imports |
   191→
   192→---
   193→
   194→## 10. Project Structure
   195→
   196→```
   197→app/
   198→├── (marketing)/     # Route group
   199→│   └── page.tsx
   200→├── (dashboard)/
   201→│   ├── layout.tsx   # Dashboard layout
   202→│   └── page.tsx
   203→├── api/
   204→│   └── [resource]/
   205→│       └── route.ts
   206→└── components/
   207→    └── ui/
   208→```
   209→
   210→---
   211→
   212→> **Remember:** Server Components are the default for a reason. Start there, add client only when needed.
   213→
